question: Your engine is getting close to needing an overhaul, you've noticed your fuel consumption is about 7% higher than the POH value of 9.2 gph. If the repair shop is 173 nautical miles away, how much fuel will you burn flying your plane to the shop for a new engine? Your average groundspeed is 103 knots.

The true course is 223°, the variation is 5° east, and the winds are from 311° at 22 knots. If your IAS is 133 mph at 4,500' MSL with an OAT of 22°C and altimeter setting of 1013 hPa, what is your groundspeed?
What is the density altitude at Jackson Hole (elev. 6,451') in July when the temperature is 86°F and the altimeter is 29.88"?

Using the following information, how far out can you fly and loiter for 30 min, and then return to your departure airport with 30 min of fuel remaining? [[NEXT_LINE]] IAS: 95 knots [[NEXT_LINE]] Wind: 115° true at 38 knots [[NEXT_LINE]] Fuel burn: 9.3 gph [[NEXT_LINE]] TC out: 075° [[NEXT_LINE]] Fuel tank capacity: 65 gallons useable [[NEXT_LINE]] Altitude: 4,500 MSL (standard day)

You have loaded up your plane with 4 passengers and are right at the max weight of 3,115 pounds. You weigh 170 pounds and will be the PIC for today's flight. You have Bob in the front seat (arm 37") with you and he weighs 225 pounds. In the back (arm 118"), you have Sally (115 pounds) and Jane (155 pounds). Your CG is a bit too far forward, 3 inches beyond the limit. What must you do to bring the CG within limits?
choice_d: It just won't work without ballast.
correct_choice: It just won't work without ballast.

question: You are on a 320 SM cross-country flight. You notice that you seem to be a bit off course, and after locating yourself you find you are 4.3 NM off course just a quarter of the way into your flight. How many degrees must you turn to arrive on course 10 NM before your destination?
How long will it take to fly from LAX to EWR at M 0.785 at FL370 on a standard day if the winds are from 299° true at 102 mph. The true course is 078° and the distance is 2,732 SM.

You wish to check your actual groundspeed against your flight planned groundspeed. You planned to have a groundspeed of 102 knots, but you see that you crossed 3 statute miles in 1 min 28 seconds. How far off your planned groundspeed are you?

You're about to head off on a short ski trip with friends and have finished your weight and balance calculations. Your plane weighs 3,188 pounds and its CG is 44.12". Just before you get ready to start the engines, one of your friends realized he left his bag of clothes in the car. If he adds a 38 pound bag to station 122.2", what is your new CG?
The pressure altitude is 10,500 feet with a temp of -20°C. Your indicated altitude is 10,000 feet and the station altitude is 5,000 feet. What is your true altitude?

Your GPS shows your magnetic track is 102° while you hold a magnetic heading of 110° and your groundspeed is 166 knots. What are the magnetic winds aloft if your IAS is 164 knots at 6,000 feet with a temperature of +5°C and the altimeter set at 29.92"?

The missed approach climb gradient requires a minimum gradient of 350 ft./nautical mile. If your indicated airspeed on the missed approach will be 170 knots, which results in a climb rate of 1,050 ft/min, will you meet the climb gradient requirements at 7,500 feet pressure altitude on a standard day?
choice_a: Yes, with 20 feet/nautical mile to spare
choice_b: No, you will be 18 feet/nautical mile short
choice_c: Yes, with just 4 feet/nautical mile to spare
choice_d: No, you will be just 3 feet/nautical mile short
correct_choice: No, you will be 18 feet/nautical mile short

question: If you are cruising at FL280 and 225 knots calibrated airspeed, by how many degrees would the indicated temperature be higher than actual outside air temperature if the true airspeed is 336 knots? Assume your air data computer uses a recovery coefficient of 1.0.
You require 12° of wind correction to maintain your ground track of 357° at 77 KTAS. How much crosswind do you have if winds aloft are 140° at 27 knots?
Find an aircraft's groundspeed given the following: Winds aloft are 335° at 18 knots, true course of 202°, and true airspeed of 174 knots.
How many gallons of fuel does an aircraft burn during a flight of 3 hours 37 minutes if it averages 8.4 GPH?
Assuming calm wind, how long does it take to fly a 12 DME arc counterclockwise from the 133° radial to the 048° radial at 94 KTAS?

The flight from Oshkosh, WI to Columbus, OH is 339 NM on a true course of 132°. With a cruise TAS of 129 knots and winds aloft at 7,500' MSL of 085° at 24 knots, how far along in the flight would the time to return to Oshkosh equal the time remaining to Columbus?
choice_d: Halfway into the 3 hour 3 minute flight

question: Referencing the previous question, the eastern shore of Lake Michigan is 202 NM from Columbus. Where would the equal time point be in relation to the shore?
choice_c: Prior to crossing over the shore
context: Referenced question 18: The flight from Oshkosh, WI to Columbus, OH is 339 NM on a true course of 132°. With a cruise TAS of 129 knots and winds aloft at 7,500' MSL of 085° at 24 knots, how far along in the flight would the time to return to Oshkosh equal the time remaining to Columbus?

question: Referencing the previous two questions, what is the groundspeed if the pilot decided to turn back to Oshkosh?

question: Your landing ground roll increases 10% for every 4 kt increase in airspeed at touchdown. If you normally require 560' when touching down at 36 kts, how much runway will you use if you land at 44 kts?

Your aircraft weighs 2,060 lbs with a CG of 13.7". You want to load baggage, located at area 77", until you reach the aircraft's maximum gross weight of 2,500 lbs. What will your CG be after you are loaded to the aircraft's maximum gross weight?
You are departing runway 32. If the winds are 337@16, what is the crosswind component?
You are flying a 045° heading at 125 kts and notice you are holding a 6° left wind correction angle. You then turn to 010° but are now drifting left 4°. What are the winds aloft?
Your current prop has an 80" diameter. If you change to an 83" diameter prop, what would the blade tip speed be at 2,700 RPM? Assume standard conditions at sea level.

You decide to do a groundspeed check while tracking your progress with a nearby VOR. It takes you 4:54 to fly between the 005° and 350° radials. If your DME reads 35 nm, what is your actual groundspeed?

Your aircraft's empty weight/CG is 1734 lbs/32.7". You weigh 177 lbs and sit in the pilot's seat at an arm of 31". With 22 gallons of fuel at 39" and 10 lbs of baggage at 68", what is your ramp weight/CG?

An aircraft is passing over you at FL340, flying a true course of 139° at 0.78M. You are traveling in the opposite direction at FL330 and 0.83M. The wind is 290@94 kts, and temperature is ISA. What is your closure rate with the other aircraft?

While flying visually along a road, you want to check the winds. You know that the road runs exactly 090°/270° true course (4° W variation), and you are flying east. Your compass shows a heading of 081°, and the correction card says "TO FLY 090 STEER 088". If it takes you 49 seconds to travel between mile markers and your airspeed indicator shows 83 kts, what are the true winds aloft? Since you are flying at low altitude, assume IAS=TAS.

In your aircraft, you can cruise at 154 mph TAS while burning 13.1 gph, or 133 mph TAS while burning 8.9 gph. Your true course today is 338°, winds aloft are 036@29 kts, and you have 34 gallons of fuel. How much additional range do you gain by flying at the slower speed assuming no reserve fuel?

You are planning a flight at 6500' with a true course of 049° at 137 KTAS. Winds aloft are 290@33 kts. If the flight is 314 km, how long after departure will the time to continue equal the time to return?
You are taking off from an airport at an elevation of 1,259 meters, the altimeter setting is 29.68", and the temperature is 83°F. What is your density altitude?
Your friend asks you to show him what 1.8g feels like. You don't have a g-meter in your aircraft, so as you roll into a level turn, what bank angle do you need to maintain?
Your aircraft cruises at 114 KTAS, and can carry 4 people. Disregarding wind, what is your per passenger fuel economy if you burn 9.2 gallons per hour of fuel?
Your aircraft weighs 1097 kgs empty. If you put 128 liters of fuel in the tanks, what percentage of the total weight is comprised of fuel?
How many kilograms of 100LL avgas does it take to fill up your aircraft's 48 U.S. gallon tanks?

You calculate the weight and balance of your C-172SP with yourself (157 lbs.) and your friend Annie (133 lbs.) in the front seats (arm 37 inches). Sebastian, your little pony at 274 lbs., is in the baggage compartment (arm 95 inches). You have a gross weight of 2378 lbs with a CG of 46.7 inches. Sebastian is a little cramped in the baggage compartment, so you move him to the back seat (arm 73 inches). How does the CG change?
The runway heading is 267°. The ATIS reported winds are 230° at 22 kts. What heading must you fly to track the centerline down final if your TAS on final is 156 km/hr?

The winds at your cruising altitude are forecasted to be 135° at 42 kts. You are flying a true course of 335°. Your destination is 486 nm from your departure point. How many gallons of fuel will you burn and what true heading will you fly on this flight? Your Cruise Master 100 burns 110.4 pph of 100LL gasoline and averages a TAS of 192 mph.

You are flying a true course of 065°, and you lost the printout of the winds aloft forecast. You are flying a TAS of 134 kts. You initially fly heading 075° and drift 5° right of course. You correct and fly heading 055° and drift 2° left. What are the winds aloft?
